title: classifyCurrentSession purity claim is unimplementable for SaveFailedState without external Note source
description: The verification architecture (line 15) classifies classifyCurrentSession as 'Pure core' with 'No ports, no side effects, deterministic'. PROP-EPNS-001 verifies referential transparency. However, as identified in FIND-SPEC-001, SaveFailedState does not carry a Note. If the implementation needs to retrieve the Note from an external source (e.g., a note repository or the editor's current buffer), classifyCurrentSession can no longer be pure. This undermines the entire purity boundary design for Step 1. The proof obligation PROP-EPNS-001 would be unfalsifiable because the function's purity depends on its input type carrying all needed data.

evidence.snippet: | Step 1 | classifyCurrentSession | **Pure core** | Accepts only EditingSessionState; returns CurrentSessionDecision; no ports, no side effects, deterministic. Property-test target. |

recommendation: This finding is a downstream consequence of FIND-SPEC-001. Once SaveFailedState is amended to carry Note, the purity claim becomes valid. No separate fix needed beyond resolving FIND-SPEC-001.
